unit.name: deprecation and native error signaling fixture harness
unit.public_contract: WordPress 7.0 deprecation helpers, wp_trigger_error(), wp_die(), and selected wp_die handlers keep native PHP warning, deprecation, exception, output, and hook behavior while the candidate side is still an oracle source mirror.

native_boundaries.reason: E_USER_WARNING, E_USER_NOTICE, and E_USER_DEPRECATED are PHP engine-level trigger_error() events; fixtures record type and message through a native error handler.
WordPress maps E_USER_ERROR in wp_trigger_error() to the native WP_Exception class.
wp_die handlers combine PHP output buffering, header/status calls, and optional die()/exit behavior.
Deprecation, _doing_it_wrong(), wp_trigger_error(), and wp_die handler selection all expose native PHP callback hooks and filters.

notes: This slice intentionally records an external-oracle fixture before promoting any error/deprecation implementation to Haxe. A later candidate should isolate message construction and branch decisions while keeping PHP-native signaling, headers, and die/exit behavior behind explicit boundaries.
